segments.text: Thousands of bird die from wind turbines every year. Why? Because the blade spins so fast they become invisible and birds
can't see them until it's too late. So engineers built cameras with AI to watch every bird. When they come close,
turbine shut down automatically. This idea seemed perfect, but only 5% fewer birds died. So they got a better idea.
This time, instead of stopping the turbines, they just slowed them down.
Blades still spin, but slower and safer for birds. This time 15% fewer birds died. So they wanted more. And here is
when the real breakthrough came. One designer thought differently. No cameras, no technology, just one blade among the rest. While others spin at 170
mph and blur together. That single blade breaks the pattern. Birds see it, they recognize danger, and then they fly away. Result: 70% fewer birds.
